items.title: GPT-5.5-Cyber built a zlib fuzzing lab in a day
items.summary: We’re running Patch the Planet, an ongoing collaboration with OpenAI that pairs Trail of Bits engineers directly with more than 30 open-source projects. Its goal is to front-run a serious problem facing open-source maintainers: highly capable models like GPT-5.5-Cyber will soon c

items.title: Bitcoin’s BIP 110 fork deadline nears with miner support at zero
items.summary: The BIP 110 proposal would cap arbitrary data on Bitcoin for a year, but Michael Saylor, Adam Back and others say turning a spam dispute into a consensus fight could create a bigger risk than the spam itself.

items.title: AI found an Ethereum bug that could take validators offline, but humans had to prove it
items.summary: The Ethereum Foundation pointed coordinated AI agents at the software its validators run and got a remotely triggerable crash out of it. It also got a pile of confident, well-written findings that were not bugs at all.

items.title: Mutation testing comes to DAML
items.summary: In April we released Mewt, our open-source mutation-testing engine that finds the gaps in your test suite. Today we’re expanding it with support for DAML, the language Canton Network applications are written in. Mewt now reads DAML, generates several classes of mutants (including

items.title: Paradigm shifts vs bubbles: AI chips and bitcoin show powerful trends can still produce severe corrections
items.summary: Structural change can create lasting opportunities, but explosive rallies in semiconductors, metals and bitcoin show how quickly strong narratives can become speculative excess.

items.title: U.S. inflation, second-quarter earnings reports: Crypto Week Ahead
items.summary: Your look at what's coming in the week starting July 13.

items.title: Live markets: Bitcoin slips below $63,000 in an Asian-session leverage flush
items.summary: The liquidations were minor, running at about a sixth of what the market saw at its worst over the past 30 days, per CoinGlass.

items.title: Bitcoin holds near $63,800 as war-driven selloff hits everything but crypto
items.summary: Gold, oil, stocks and bonds all moved sharply on the fourth round of U.S. strikes on Iran, but bitcoin is little-changed.

items.title: Japan stablecoin payments advance with Lawson trial, Netstars launch
items.summary: Lawson will test yen stablecoin payments in Tokyo as Netstars launches a merchant service supporting USDC, USDT and JPYC.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: AI microbusinesses could drive $262B in stablecoin volume by 2033: Swyftx
items.summary: The AI-native cohort of the expanding gig economy could increasingly use stablecoins to avoid slow and expensive traditional payment rails, Australian crypto exchange Swyftx said.

items.title: Bank of Thailand targets USDT and cash flows in gray money crackdown
items.summary: Thailand has been plagued by Chinese-affiliated scam centers, with illicit gains flowing through a “gray money” economy.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs draw $197M, snap 8-week outflow streak
items.summary: Analysts are not yet ready to call it a recovery in institutional demand for Bitcoin.

items.title: Signs of life?: State of Crypto
items.summary: Several sources told CoinDesk that a new draft of the Clarity Act may drop this week, but challenges remain.

items.title: Stablecoin market cap has shrunk by $10 billion since May, but analyst sees no reason to panic
items.summary: The market shrank by $7.7 billion in June alone, the largest dollar amount since May 2022's Terra-Luna crash, but stablecoins will likely resume their long-term growth, one analyst said.

items.title: Bitcoin is nearing a power law support line Fidelity has tracked since 2015
items.summary: The group's Dir. of Global Macro Jurien Timmer calls it an accumulation zone but notes the lack of a catalyst to bounce yet.

items.title: Bitcoin, ether little changed as U.S. launches fresh Iran strikes
items.summary: The U.S. hit Iran for the third time this week and Tehran has reportedly closed the Strait of Hormuz again.

items.title: Ripple once weighed shutting down and handing XRP to shareholders, CEO says
items.summary: Ripple's Brad Garlinghouse says he and co-founder Chris Larsen considered winding the company down and handing its XRP to shareholders before deciding to fight the 2020 lawsuit.

items.title: Robinhood L2 sparks ETH optimism, Saylor 'muddies waters.' Hodler's Digest, July 5-12, 2026
items.summary: Even Ethereum's critics say Robinhood Chain is bullish for ETH. Nigel Farage and US President Donald Trump become embroiled in crypto related scandals, and more.

items.title: Strategy's Saylor needs clarity in BTC pivot message to convince investors: StanChart
items.summary: Standard Chartered sees communication challenges facing the biggest digital asset treasury company as "muddying the waters" for Bitcoin in the near term.
